title: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR FERMENTED NONI FRUIT JUICE North America and South West Pacific CXS 356R-2023

sections.section: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR FERMENTED NONI FRUIT JUICE North America and South West Pacific CXS 356R-2023
sections.summary: Document title: Regional Standard for Fermented Noni Fruit Juice (CXS 356R-2023) — the heading of the standard.
Non-substantive adoption note indicating the standard was adopted in 2023.

This standard applies to fermented noni fruit juice used as a food or food ingredient and explicitly excludes non-fermented noni juice, other noni products (from leaves, bark or flowers), and noni products intended for medicinal purposes.

Defines fermented noni fruit juice as derived from fermenting fresh Morinda citrifolia L. var. citrifolia; specifies raw fruit should be fresh, firm, mature to ripe, washed and dried with over‑ripe, fallen, damaged or foreign-matter‑contaminated fruit rejected; and states whole fruit or pulp may be fermented (spontaneously or with starter), juice extracted and then pasteurized or otherwise treated to eliminate pathogens.
Product definition: fermented noni fruit juice is the juice obtained by fermenting fresh fruits of Morinda citrifolia L. var. citrifolia (Rubiaceae).

Specifies raw material requirements for noni fruit: fresh, firm, mature to ripe (greenish‑yellow to white), harvested, washed and dried, optionally crushed to pulp (excluding seeds), and excludes use of over‑ripe, fallen, green, bruised, damaged fruit or fruit containing foreign materials.

Describes processing: whole fruit or pulp may be fermented spontaneously or with starter cultures, juice is extracted from the fermented product, and the resulting fermented noni fruit juice is pasteurized or otherwise treated to eliminate pathogens of public health significance.

Sets composition and quality criteria for fermented noni fruit juice: minimum soluble solids (Brix) 5.5°, pH 3.5–3.9, ethanol <0.5% v/v, presence of deacetylasperulosidic acid and scopoletin, and requires the product to be free from objectionable matter (e.g., leaves, seed or skin fragments, stems, insects) in accordance with good manufacturing practice.

Products must comply with Codex maximum levels for contaminants and toxins (General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed) and with Codex-established maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Recommends that fermented noni fruit juice be prepared and handled per the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1-1969) and other relevant Codex texts, and that the product comply with any microbiological criteria established under CXG 21-1997.

Packaging must protect the hygienic and organoleptic quality of fermented noni fruit juice; materials must be new or food‑grade recycled, containers must meet quality, hygiene, ventilation and resistance requirements for handling and shipping, and packages must be free of foreign matter and odors.
Minimum fill requirement: the product should occupy not less than 90% of the container's water capacity (the volume of distilled water at 20 °C the sealed container will hold when completely filled).
Minimum fill requirement: the product should occupy not less than 90% of the container's water capacity (the volume of distilled water at 20 °C the sealed container will hold when completely filled).
Minimum fill requirement: the product should occupy not less than 90% of the container's water capacity (the volume of distilled water at 20 °C the sealed container will hold when completely filled).

Labelling must follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985); the product name shall be 'fermented noni fruit juice' (or a customary local term), and labelling of non‑retail containers should comply with CXS 346-2021.
The food shall be named 'fermented noni fruit juice', though the term 'noni fruit juice' may be replaced by a customary local name (e.g., 'nonu juice' or 'nono juice').
States an obligation that labelling of non-retail food containers must comply with the Codex General Standard for the Labelling of Non-Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
Requires that methods of analysis and sampling to verify compliance with this standard use the Relevant Recommended Methods of Analysis and Sampling (CXS 234-1999).
Non-substantive notes section listing bibliographic references (Codex standards and guidelines cited) and a Codex website link for member information.
